title: NOC 94143 – Testers and graders, food and beverage processing | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, as Canada’s food manufacturing sector transitions to "Precision Quality Control," Testers and Graders (NOC 94143) have become the frontline of food safety and brand integrity. These professionals conduct the critical sensory and scientific tests that ensure everything from craft beer to industrial-scale dairy products meets strict company and federal standards. While classified as TEER 4, the role is increasingly technical, requiring operators to use digital sensors and automated data-logging systems. With a high retirement rate and a "Balanced" national outlook, international workers with experience in industrial quality control are seeing steady demand across all provinces.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Category4 (Usually requiring secondary school and on-the-job training)Major Group94 – Machine operators, assemblers and inspectorsSub-Major Group941 – Machine operators in mineral and metal processing

common_job_titles: Product Tester – Food and Beverage Processing
duties: Sampling: Taking product samples at various stages of the production cycle for immediate testing.
Sensory Examination: Inspecting ingredients and finished products by sight, touch, taste, and smell to ensure they meet quality benchmarks.
Scientific Testing: Conducting routine tests for specifications like alcohol content, carbonation, moisture, fat content, and temperature.
Grading & Sorting: Categorizing raw materials (like beef or fish) into different grades based on weight, color, and quality.
Compliance Oversight: Ensuring that all plant hygiene and sanitation practices conform to federal (CFIA) and company policies.
2026 Update: Operating digital "Traceability Scanners" to log quality data directly into blockchain-secured supply chain systems.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Experience: Previous experience as a machine operator or labourer in the food processing industry is often preferred.
Training: On-the-job training is standard and provided by the employer.
Skills: Strong sensory perception, attention to detail, and basic computer literacy for data entry.
job_outlook: The Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS) expects a "Balanced" market. While some routine sorting is being automated, the "Human Sensory" aspect of food testing remains irreplaceable. Retirement is the primary driver of job openings, creating over 4,000 openings over the next decade.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook for testers and graders is steady, with high demand in specialized food hubs:
Ontario: Good prospects. Demand is concentrated in the Greater Golden Horseshoe, supporting the province's massive dairy, bakery, and beverage clusters.
British Columbia: Stable demand. Focused in the Lower Mainland and Okanagan Valley, particularly for the wine, craft brewery, and fruit processing sectors.
Alberta & Saskatchewan: Strong demand. Essential for the quality control of beef, poultry, and value-added grain products in Canada's agricultural heartland.
Nova Scotia & New Brunswick: Good prospects. High seasonal and year-round demand in fish plants and fruit processing facilities outside of major urban centers.
Why Work in Canada as a Food Tester or Grader?
Safety-Critical Impact: You are the final check before products reach consumers, playing a vital role in public health and food safety.
Advanced QA Technology: Work with 2026-grade tools, including automated pH meters, digital colorimeters, and AI-assisted defect detection software.
Path to PR: Frequently eligible for Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) semi-skilled streams and the 2026 TR to PR transition streams.
Defined Career Ladder: Progress into NOC 92012 (Supervisors) or specialize further as a Food processing quality control technician.
pathways: TR to PR Pathway (2026): Food testers are classified as "Essential Workers," making them high-priority candidates for this 33,000-person transition quota.
Saskatchewan Agriculture Talent Pathway: A specialized stream that includes food processing roles for workers with a job offer.
Ontario In-Demand Skills Stream: Frequently includes NOC 94143 for those with a valid job offer in Ontario’s food manufacturing sector.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-success pathway for those with job offers in the Atlantic provinces' food and fish processing sectors.
